title: The World as a Divine Body — Sallustius and the Recovery of Sacred Matter
author: Sallustius / Thomas Taylor

body.text: The world is a living production of divine power. Its endurance and order are signs that it remains suspended from incorruptible causes. Its beauty is a third sign, woven through both like a thread pulled tight. The spiritual path begins by perceiving matter as generated and held.

If therefore the gods produced the world by art, they would not cause it simply to be, but to be in some particular manner; for all art produces form. From whence therefore does the world derive its being? If from nature, since nature in fabricating imparts something of itself to its productions and the gods are incorporeal, it is necessary that the world, the offspring of the gods, should be incorporeal. It remains therefore that the gods produced the world by power alone.

The gods produce the world by power alone — a continuous generation. Craftsmen stand at a distance from their work, severed from what they make. A world generated by divine power remains joined to its source.

body.caption: The macrocosm as continuous production — the world held in divine power at every level.

body.text: Productions of this kind cannot be destroyed unless the producing cause is deprived of power. So that those who subject the world to corruption plainly deny that there are gods; or if they assert that there are gods, they deprive divinity of power.
The world endures because it is held. Forms arise and pass — the producing power continues. The eye must learn to distinguish surface alteration from underlying procession.

body.caption: The held world — divine power as continuous ground of all becoming.

body.text: But from all these nothing happens to the gods beyond what they already possess; for what accession can be made to a divine nature? But a conjunction with our souls and the gods is by this means produced.
Ritual forms receptivity. A consecrated rite reorients the soul and opens it. Sacred action changes the condition of participation.

body.caption: The rite as conjunction — sacred action that aligns the soul with what is already present.

body.text: Neither are they separated from the first cause, nor from each other, nor from things posterior to themselves. For as sensible natures are not separated from the gods, so neither are the gods from the first cause.
First cause, gods, souls, bodies, rites, symbols — distinct levels of one order. Each level exists by relation. The visible carries the trace of what exceeds it.
Matter is the body spirit has already shaped for itself. The imaginal enterprise begins here: render matter as dependent radiance. The visible world is spirit's first body.
